title: Environment, Shell State, Startup Files, and Sessions · Operations Case: Startup File Order
summary: Repair a command that works interactively but fails in automation by tracing environment and startup differences instead of adding global hacks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux startup file order increment in an environment-diff and startup-resolution notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify login, interactive, non-interactive, and remote shell startup paths before editing configuration.
Trace aliases, functions, builtins, hashed commands, PATH search, and explicit paths with type and command evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Startup File Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — check-report works in an interactive shell but fails from a service with PATH=/usr/bin:/bin, LANG=C.UTF-8, and HOME=/var/lib/lab. Trace command resolution and startup files without exporting a secret token.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Startup File Order contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify login, interactive, non-interactive, and remote shell startup paths before editing configuration.
